title: KeyPackage serialized as serde_json, not TLS -- non-standard wire format
description: mls.rs key_package_bytes() uses serde_json::to_vec(kpb.key_package()) and add_member() uses serde_json::from_slice(). The comment says 'KeyPackage only derives TlsDeserialize under test-utils feature'. This means the KeyPackage on the wire is JSON, not the TLS-encoded format defined in RFC 9420. The relay stores it opaquely (store.rs:save_key_package), which is correct. But this creates a non-standard wire format that will be incompatible with any RFC 9420-compliant implementation when Phase 4 (Android client) arrives. An Android MLS stack will produce TLS-encoded KeyPackages that the Rust client cannot parse with serde_json. The divergence should be fixed before any multi-implementation scenario. Fix: add the 'test-utils' feature to openmls (accept the build overhead) and use TLS serialization, or manually implement TLS encode/decode for KeyPackage.
notes: CONFIRMED from openmls key_packages/mod.rs: KeyPackage derives TlsSize but NOT TlsSerialize or TlsDeserialize. KeyPackageIn (the deserialization wrapper) derives TlsDeserialize. There is no standard way to TLS-serialize a KeyPackage owned value -- TlsSerialize is simply not implemented. The mls.rs comment 'only derives TlsDeserialize under test-utils' is misleading: the real situation is that KeyPackage has no TlsSerialize at all, not a feature gate issue. The serde_json workaround is the only option given current openmls API. The wire format incompatibility with RFC 9420 compliant implementations (Android, Phase 4) remains real.
